overnight_news: **Trump's announcement on US risk insurance for Persian Gulf maritime trade (esp. Strait of Hormuz)**: Eased oil supply fears after Iran tensions, helping stocks rebound from intraday lows; impacts energy sector broadly (oil prices +4.7%).[1][4]

**Oil prices spike nearly 5% (Brent to $81, WTI to $74) on US-Iran/Middle East tensions**: Fuels inflation worries, delays Fed rate cut expectations to September; hits industrials (-2%), materials (-2.7%).[1][4]
**Dow/S&P/Nasdaq rebound sharply (Dow +0.49%, S&P +0.78%, Nasdaq +1.29%)**: Futures turned positive pre-market after volatile session, signaling improved sentiment for tomorrow.[1][2][7]
**Upcoming ADP private payrolls report (est. +48K Feb jobs)**: Key labor data could sway Fed policy views amid inflation/oil risks.[1]
**Earnings beats: Broadcom (AVGO), Okta (OKTA), Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF)**: Reported ahead of expectations, boosting tech/consumer stocks in rebound.[1][7]
